name: T-Mobile Visa

verdict.text: Only makes sense for active T-Mobile customers — rewards aren't cashable

earn_rates.category: Phones, devices & accessories — T-Mobile stores, T-Mobile.com & the T-Life app

earn_note: Capital One and T-Mobile's own terms exclude device equipment installment plans, wireless service fees, and prepaid service from the 5% device rate — the 5% is for buying a phone or accessory outright, not for paying your monthly bill. There's no cap on either rate and rewards don't expire for the life of the account. No standing sign-up bonus at launch; a limited-time $100 bonus (after $500 in non-T-Mobile spend within 90 days) has circulated with an application deadline around July 23, 2026 — confirm it's still live before counting on it.

redemption_options.method: T-Mobile wireless bill, or phone/device/accessory purchases at T-Mobile.com, in the T-Life app, or in-store

points.per_100k: $1,000 toward a T-Mobile bill or device — no other redemption exists
points.note: A closed-loop currency, not cash back — there's no statement credit, gift card, or Capital One redemption option. Balances don't expire while the account is open, but they're stranded if you ever leave T-Mobile.

transfer_partners.highlight: No transfer partners — T-Mobile Rewards are a closed-loop currency good only at T-Mobile, not Capital One miles or transferable points.

insurance.detail: $500/claim · $50,000/cardholder · 90 days (standard Capital One Visa Signature Purchase Security benefit)
+1 yr on warranties ≤3 yrs · up to $10,000/claim, $50,000/cardholder
Up to $250,000 · when fare is charged entirely to the card
Not included — a notable gap given this is a phone carrier's own card

protection_note: These figures come from Capital One's standard Visa Signature Guide to Benefits, since a T-Mobile-specific guide wasn't independently confirmed — treat them as the baseline you should get, not a guarantee. The most surprising omission is cell phone protection: despite being T-Mobile's own card, it doesn't insure the phone you're paying it off with. There's no trip cancellation/interruption or baggage delay coverage either, only lost-luggage reimbursement.
rental_note: Secondary auto rental collision/theft coverage via Visa Signature, up to 15 days domestically or 31 days internationally — decline the counter's own CDW/LDW and charge the entire rental to this card to activate it; pays out after your personal auto policy.

services.name: No annual fee
services.detail: $0/year, with a variable 18.49%–28.49% purchase APR based on creditworthiness.

services.name: T-Mobile AutoPay discount
services.detail: $5/month per line (up to 8 lines) off an eligible T-Mobile wireless plan when AutoPay is set to this card — separate from, and stackable with, the T-Mobile Rewards earned on purchases.

services.name: No foreign transaction fees
services.detail: $0 on purchases made outside the US.

services.name: Visa Signature perks
services.detail: 24/7 Visa Signature Concierge, Visa Signature Luxury Hotel Collection, and retailer shopping discounts.

services.name: Eligibility is carrier-locked
services.detail: Open only to active T-Mobile postpaid consumer customers with at least one line on an eligible smartphone plan; T-Mobile for Business, Prepaid, and Government accounts don't qualify.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards

benefits.text: Earns T-Mobile Rewards on their spend (into your account)
Same purchase protections as the primary card

additional_cards.note: Free to add, but only useful for a household member also on the T-Mobile plan, since the rewards can't be redeemed outside T-Mobile.

timeline.text: Capital One and T-Mobile launch the T-Mobile Visa, the carrier's first co-branded credit card — 5% back on T-Mobile device purchases and 2% elsewhere, redeemable only within the T-Mobile ecosystem, with no annual fee.
